task.title: Protecting the IPsec NSS database with a password

li.p: The /var/lib/ipsec/nss/ directory contains the NSS database.

context.p: By default, only the root user can access the IPsec Network Security Services (NSS) database in the /var/lib/ipsec/nss/ directory. You can additionally protect the database with a password. This is required if you run RHEL in Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) mode.

step.cmd: Enable password protection for the Libreswan NSS database:

info.codeblock: Enter Password or Pin for "NSS Certificate DB": <password>
info.p: If the database is currently not protected by a password, press Enter.

step.cmd: To unlock the database, the ipsec service requires the /etc/ipsec.d/nsspassword file. Create the file with the following content:

li.p: If the host does not run in FIPS mode:
If the host runs in FIPS mode:

step.cmd: Set secure permissions on the /etc/ipsec.d/nsspassword file:

li.p: Verify that the ipsec service is running:
If the command returns active, the service successfully uses the password file to unlock the NSS database.
Perform an action on the NSS database that requires the password. For example, display the private keys:
li.codeblock: # certutil -K -d /var/lib/ipsec/nss/ certutil: Checking token "NSS Certificate DB" in slot "NSS User Private Key and Certificate Services" Enter Password or Pin for "NSS Certificate DB":
li.p: Verify that the command prompts for the password.
